signals.headline: US Announces Naval Blockade of Strait of Hormuz After Iran Talks Collapse | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Announces Naval Blockade of Strait of Hormuz After Iran Talks Collapse | YES | HIGH confidence**

US President Donald Trump announced a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the US and Iran in Islamabad failed to produce an agreement.[1][2] The blockade began on April 13, 2026, targeting all maritime traffic to and from Iranian ports.[2]

signals.headline: Trump Threatens Immediate Military Action Against Iranian Vessels | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Trump Threatens Immediate Military Action Against Iranian Vessels | YES | HIGH confidence**

Trump claimed Iranian naval assets had been "obliterated" and warned of immediate strikes on any approaching vessels, stating that "any Iranian who fires at us or at peaceful vessels will be blown to hell."[1][2]

signals.headline: Ceasefire Remains Fragile as Israel Continues Operations in Lebanon | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ceasefire Remains Fragile as Israel Continues Operations in Lebanon | YES | HIGH confidence**

On day five of the ceasefire, Israel continued attacking Lebanon despite planned talks between the two countries scheduled for Tuesday.[1][3] The broader regional ceasefire is showing significant strain with failed diplomacy and military escalation.

signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 annual inflation rose to 2.2% | NO | HIGH confidence**  
This figure, released around early April 2026, increased from 2.0% in January-February but fell short of market forecasts of 2.4%, signaling contained price pressures amid global energy volatility from the Iran conflict.[1]

signals.headline: Iran Warns Blockade is "Illegal" and Threatens Regional Escalation | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Iran Warns Blockade is "Illegal" and Threatens Regional Escalation | YES | HIGH confidence**

The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (I.R.G.C.) called Washington's blockade "illegal" and an "act of piracy," warning that if Iranian ports are threatened, no port in the region would remain safe.[2] Tehran signaled it could tighten control over the Strait of Hormuz itself, a vital global oil route.[2]
